q: In which 1385 battle did Portugal secure independence from Castile?
exp: The victory led to the building of the Batalha Monastery.

q: Which Portuguese king vanished at Alcácer-Quibir in 1578?
exp: His disappearance gave rise to the 'Sebastianism' myth.

q: In which year was the Republic proclaimed in Portugal?
Which 1494 treaty divided the world between Portugal and Castile?
exp: The Tordesillas line later gave Brazil to Portugal.

q: What is the world's oldest diplomatic alliance still in force?
exp: It dates back to the Treaty of Windsor of 1386.

q: Which dynasty took the throne with the 1640 Restoration of Independence?
exp: John IV was acclaimed king on 1 December 1640.

q: In which year did Portugal join the then EEC (now the EU)?
exp: Portugal and Spain joined together on 1 January 1986.

q: Which Portuguese navigator first rounded the Cape of Good Hope?
exp: He rounded the cape in 1488, opening the sea route to India.

q: Which Portuguese writer won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1998?
exp: He is the only Portuguese-language author to win the Literature Nobel.

q: Who led the 'Estado Novo' dictatorship for decades?
Which navigator led the first expedition to sail around the world?
exp: The Portuguese-born Magellan set off in 1519 for Spain.

q: Which Portuguese explorer reached Brazil in 1500?
exp: Cabral landed in Brazil on 22 April 1500.
